content.problemStatement:
If I keep moving, they won't know |
I'll morph to someone else |
What they throw at me's too slow |
I'll morph to someone else |
I'm just a ghost |
I'll morph to someone else |
Defense mechanism mode!

You are given two integer sequences A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč and B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1‚Äč,B2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äč and an integer KKK. You may perform the following operation at most 3‚čÖN3 \cdot N3‚čÖN times:


Select a contiguous subsequence of AAA with length KKK. Let's denote this subsequence by AL,AL+1,‚Ä¶,AL+K‚ąí1A_L, A_{L+1}, \ldots, A_{L+K-1}AL‚Äč,AL+1‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AL+K‚ąí1‚Äč.
Create an integer sequence S1,S2,‚Ä¶,SKS_1, S_2, \ldots, S_KS1‚Äč,S2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,SK‚Äč which is isomorphic to the selected subsequence ‚Äē for each pair of integers iii and jjj (1‚Č§i,j‚Č§K1 \le i, j \le K1‚Č§i,j‚Č§K), Si=SjS_i = S_jSi‚Äč=Sj‚Äč must hold if AL‚ąí1+i=AL‚ąí1+jA_{L-1+i} = A_{L-1+j}AL‚ąí1+i‚Äč=AL‚ąí1+j‚Äč and Si‚Č†SjS_i \neq S_jSi‚ÄčÓÄ†=Sj‚Äč must hold if AL‚ąí1+i‚Č†AL‚ąí1+jA_{L-1+i} \neq A_{L-1+j}AL‚ąí1+i‚ÄčÓÄ†=AL‚ąí1+j‚Äč. In addition, 1‚Č§Si‚Č§1,0001 \le S_i \le 1,0001‚Č§Si‚Äč‚Č§1,000 must hold for each valid iii.
For each valid iii, replace AL‚ąí1+iA_{L-1+i}AL‚ąí1+i‚Äč by SiS_iSi‚Äč.
Is it possible to change the sequence AAA to BBB? If it is possible, find a sequence of operations which morphs AAA to BBB.
constraints: 1‚Č§Ai,Bi‚Č§1,0001 \le A_i, B_i \le 1,0001‚Č§Ai‚Äč,Bi‚Äč‚Č§1,000 for each valid iii

`Explanation**Example case 1:** The state of the sequence after each operation is [1,2,3,4]‚Üí[4,3,2,4]‚Üí[4,3,2,1][1, 2, 3, 4] \rightarrow [4, 3, 2, 4] \rightarrow [4, 3, 2, 1][1,2,3,4]‚Üí[4,3,2,4]‚Üí[4,3,2,1].
See the following animation for more clarity:
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1‚Äč,B2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äč.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case:


If it is impossible to morph AAA to BBB using no more than 3‚čÖN3 \cdot N3‚čÖN operations, print a single line containing the string `"NO"` (without quotes).
Otherwise, start by printing a line containing the string `"YES"` (without quotes).
Then, print a line containing a single integer PPP (0‚Č§P‚Č§3‚čÖN0 \le P \le 3 \cdot N0‚Č§P‚Č§3‚čÖN) ‚Äē the number of operations you want to perform.
Finally, print PPP lines. Each of these lines should contain K+1K+1K+1 space-separated integers L,S1,S2,‚Ä¶,SKL, S_1, S_2, \ldots, S_KL,S1‚Äč,S2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,SK‚Äč describing an operation (1‚Č§L‚Č§N‚ąíK+11 \le L \le N-K+11‚Č§L‚Č§N‚ąíK+1; 1‚Č§Si‚Č§1,0001 \le S_i \le 1,0001‚Č§Si‚Äč‚Č§1,000 for each valid iii).
If there are multiple solutions, you may find any one of them.
